panels.title: Request rate
panels.description: Sum of `rate(aegisml_http_requests_total[5m])` — all routes, all status codes.

panels.title: Error rate (5xx share of requests)
panels.description: Ratio of 5xx responses to all requests. Alternative: `sum(rate(aegisml_http_errors_total{class="5xx"}[5m])) / sum(rate(aegisml_http_requests_total[5m]))`.

panels.title: Replica process start (Unix)
panels.description: `aegisml_process_start_timestamp_seconds` — wall clock when this pod registered metrics (align with rollout / deploy markers).

panels.title: Deployment identity (`aegisml_app_info`)
panels.description: Labels: `version`, `environment`, `git_commit`, `pod`, `namespace` (pod/namespace from Kubernetes downward API when set). Value is 1.

options.content:
**Correlate rollout to latency/errors**
1. Note **process start** time (panel above) or log line `event=service_start` with the same `git_commit` / `version`.
2. Compare **latency** and **error rate** before vs after that timestamp (Grafana time range or annotations from CI/CD).
3. Use **`aegisml_app_info`** to confirm which **version / commit / environment / pod** is serving traffic (multi-replica: one series per pod).
4. **Logs:** filter JSON fields `git_commit`, `pod_name`, `environment` — must match metrics for the same replica.

| Metric | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `aegisml_http_requests_total` | Requests by `method`, `path`, `status` |
| `aegisml_http_request_duration_seconds` | Latency histogram |
| `aegisml_http_errors_total` | `4xx` / `5xx` counts |
| `aegisml_app_info` | `version`, `environment`, `git_commit`, `pod`, `namespace` |
| `aegisml_process_start_timestamp_seconds` | Start time for this process (per replica) |
| `aegisml_predictions_*` | Inference |
